subtasks.description: Query SAM database for Slack contract renewal dates, terms, license counts, and renewal history
Query consumption data warehouse for Slack usage trends (user counts, active users, features used) over time to establish usage trajectory
Reconcile SAM renewal contracts with consumption usage trends to identify alignment gaps, forecast renewal needs, and track license utilization trajectory relative to renewal dates

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks minimum: (1) SAM renewals query is mid-tier SQL with multi-table joins across contract terms, dates, and licensing data. (2) Consumption trends query is mid-tier SQL requiring time-series aggregation and usage metrics. (3) Cross-reconciliation is frontier-tier because it requires sophisticated analysis: reconciling two data sources with different schemas, detecting growth/decline patterns in usage vs. license capacity, forecasting renewal impact, and synthesizing a coherent trajectory narrative. The frontier model is necessary to handle the multi-signal correlation and decision synthesis that "tracking trajectory" implies.

output.sql: SELECT cf.publisher, cf.product, cf.license_id, cf.fiscal_year, cf.fiscal_quarter, SUM(cf.amount_usd) AS total_spend_usd, AVG(cf.daily_active_seats) AS avg_daily_active_seats, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.event_id) AS event_count, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.user_id) AS distinct_users, inf.contract_id, inf.vendor_name, inf.invoice_date, inf.due_date, inf.paid_date, inf.amount_usd AS invoice_amount_usd, inf.status AS invoice_status FROM consumption_facts cf LEFT JOIN invoice_facts inf ON LOWER(cf.publisher) = LOWER(inf.vendor_name) WHERE LOWER(cf.publisher) LIKE '%slack%' OR LOWER(cf.product) LIKE '%slack%' GROUP BY cf.publisher, cf.product, cf.license_id, cf.fiscal_year, cf.fiscal_quarter, inf.contract_id, inf.vendor_name, inf.invoice_date, inf.due_date, inf.paid_date, inf.amount_usd, inf.status ORDER BY cf.fiscal_year ASC, cf.fiscal_quarter ASC, cf.license_id ASC

SELECT cf.fiscal_year, cf.fiscal_quarter, cf.product, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.user_id) AS distinct_users, SUM(cf.daily_active_seats) AS total_daily_active_seats, SUM(cf.amount_usd) AS total_amount_usd, COUNT(cf.event_id) AS event_count, STRING_AGG(DISTINCT cf.event_type, ', ' ORDER BY cf.event_type) AS event_types FROM consumption_facts cf WHERE cf.publisher ILIKE '%slack%' OR cf.product ILIKE '%slack%' GROUP BY cf.fiscal_year, cf.fiscal_quarter, cf.product ORDER BY cf.fiscal_year ASC, cf.fiscal_quarter ASC, cf.product ASC
